nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as insufficiently creative or impersonal
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to collaborate in creating all of my material.

nodes.label: AI memory reliability in long conversations
nodes.label_negative: AI memory loss in extended interactions
nodes.grounding_span: The models lose information if conversations go on too long.

nodes.label: AI for PowerPoint slide generation
nodes.grounding_span: I don't feel like AI can put personality into them.

nodes.label: Human irreplaceability through unique experience and perspective
nodes.grounding_span: The human component of the hands on cooking lessons is very important, and not recreateable by AI in a meaningful way.

nodes.label: Business growth and profitability
nodes.grounding_span: expanded from my original idea into a very profitable, turn key business

nodes.label: Creative control and originality
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to design the slides for the lessons.

nodes.label: Authentic human connection in teaching
nodes.grounding_span: The human interaction is important, and necessary.

nodes.label: Excitement about AI enabling business expansion
nodes.grounding_span: I was very excited when I first got started in content creation with AI.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as memory aid
nodes.grounding_span: The models lose information if conversations go on too long. It can be frustrating.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: I am very satisfied with how I already use it.

nodes.label: Ambivalence about AI taking over slide creation
nodes.grounding_span: The stuff AI creates for slides now is a little too dry and boring, or too cheesy if I ask for more personality.

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with limited back-and-forth
nodes.grounding_span: I ask for rewrites several times over until its perfect

nodes.label: Structured framework-first approach
nodes.grounding_span: The most important thing for me to create is the outline in the beginning.
